open_source.evidence: Candidate has personal GitHub repositories but no evidence of significant contributions to other people's projects. The complexity of the work is demonstrated in production experience rather than community open source.

self_projects.evidence: Projects like 'Agentic RAG over Enterprise Knowledge Graphs' demonstrate high complexity (full-stack agentic framework deployed on Kubernetes) and real-world impact. The other projects are moderately complex.

production.evidence: Strong production experience as an Intern at Nokia (ML Engineering) and Data Scientist at Maruti Suzuki, involving deploying models across 100K+ vehicles and optimizing large-scale MLOps pipelines.

technical_skills.evidence: Demonstrates strong proficiency in ML/AI frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, LLM Fine-Tuning), MLOps tools (MLflow, Optuna), distributed systems concepts (Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes), and core languages (Python, GoLang).

bonus_points.breakdown: 3 points for portfolio website (based on provided URL) + 2 points for LinkedIn profile.

deductions.reasons: No significant deductions applied. The self-projects are complex and production experience is strong.

areas_for_improvement: Increase visibility of open source contributions to demonstrate broader community engagement.
Provide more detail on the specific algorithmic complexity used in the data science projects.
Expand documentation for complex ML pipelines beyond project descriptions.

work.name: Nokia
work.summary: Knowledge Layer & RAG Architecture; Data Scientist
highlights: Designed and built the “Knowledge Layer”, a retrieval engine for querying complex technical documentation by unifying Vector Search (OpenSearch), relational metadata (PostgreSQL), and graph traversal (Neo4j).

Benchmarked 4+ SOTA embedding models against a validation corpus of 700+ technical documents. Selected Embedding Gemma for the best MRR and semantic recall. Built a custom sentence-aware chunking pipeline to optimize ingestion.
Built a Multimodal RAG pipeline to index unstructured tables and figures using a joint vector space with Jina Embeddings V4 and OpenAI CLIP, enabling text-to-visual retrieval.
Added a re-ranking stage with Qwen3 Reranker-8B (cross-encoder), measurably improving context precision in retrieved chunks.
Built an agentic workflow with the AG2 framework to automate multi-step information gathering across the knowledge base.

work.name: Maruti Suzuki (R&D Division)
work.summary: Battery Health Prediction (Predictive Maintenance); MLOps & Training Cost Reduction
highlights: Built an LSTM-based time-series classifier on telematics data to predict lead-acid battery failure in connected vehicles, trained on imbalanced sensor data using focal cross-entropy loss.
Designed a 14-day sliding window aggregation layer over raw predictions to suppress sensor noise and cut false positives before alerts reach the field.
Deployed across 100K+ connected vehicles with 98% field-validated accuracy, reducing warranty claims and roadside failures. Work resulted in a published patent (No. 202411040338).

Audited a legacy training pipeline burning $4,000/month in GPU compute. Profiled bottlenecks and migrated Random Forest workloads to optimized multi-core CPU execution, eliminating unnecessary GPU spend.
Integrated Optuna for automated hyperparameter search, cutting per-experiment wall time and total cloud compute costs.

awards.title: Patent – Automobile Battery Health Prediction System
Molecular Signatures and ML-driven Stress Biomarkers for Rainbow Trout Aquaculture

projects.name: Agentic RAG over Enterprise Knowledge Graphs
projects.description: Designed an enterprise-ready modular agentic framework (AG2) for natural-language querying over enterprise knowledge graphs spanning Ab Initio workflows. Deployed on Kubernetes, served via Vertex AI, with ZeRank2 re-ranking for retrieval precision.

projects.name: Spatiotemporal Modeling of Telematics Data Gaps
projects.description: Solved recurring multi-hour data dropouts (5% of telematics records) to regional network dead zones. Built an ML pipeline using Random Forests for anomaly classification and GRU over Spatiotemporal Sequences to map affected geographic clusters. Improved data collection completeness by 15% across connected vehicle fleet.
